content_hints.description: The Chasm Edge. Rope anchors hammered into stone by bridge crew salvagers mark the descent point. Below, darkness swallows your torchlight at thirty feet. The rock is slick with condensation and something else -- a faint bioluminescent slime that pulses in slow rhythms, like breathing.
content_hints.read_aloud: Iron pitons trail into darkness below the plateau's edge, each one hammered into stone scarred with the claw-marks of things that climb. Your torchlight dies at thirty feet -- not fading, but consumed, as if the dark below is a substance rather than an absence. Bioluminescent slime pulses on the rock face in slow rhythms. Something large shifts in the blackness. The scraping of chitin against stone echoes for far too long.

npcs.name: Salvager Drehy
npcs.dialogue: Down is easy. It is the coming back up that kills people. Mark your rope every fifty feet with a knot. When the knots stop making sense, you have gone too deep.
npcs.notes: Drehy is a former bridge crew member who found salvaging less likely to kill him. He has been deeper than any other living salvager and will not discuss what he saw below the third level.

content_hints.investigation_success: The piton placement follows a deliberate pattern -- not random handholds, but a path that avoids certain sections of the wall. Those sections are scored with claw marks from below.
content_hints.perception_success: Drehy's hands bear burn scars in the shape of rope friction. He descended in a hurry once -- fast enough to strip skin. He survived. His partner did not.
event_triggers: On entry: Something large shifts in the darkness below -- a scraping of chitin against stone that echoes for far too long. Drehy checks his rope twice.

content_hints.description: The Narrow Ledge runs along the chasm wall -- a shelf of stone barely four feet wide, polished smooth by millennia of rushing water. The left wall seeps moisture. The right drops into nothing. Cremlings scatter at your approach, their carapaces clicking against stone like a thousand tiny dice.
content_hints.read_aloud: Four feet of stone between you and eternity. The ledge is polished glass-smooth by water that has carved this path since before human memory. Moisture seeps from the wall on your left, cold and mineral-sharp. On your right, nothing -- a void so complete that dropping a stone produces no sound for seven seconds. Then it hits something. Then it hits something else. Cremlings scatter from your boot-falls, their carapaces clicking against stone like a cascade of tiny dice.

enemies.name: Cremling Swarm
enemies.notes: Swarm. Half damage from single-target attacks.

content_hints.perception_success: The cremlings are not fleeing randomly. They are retreating in formation, clearing a path along the ledge. Something trained them to move this way -- or something further down the chasm conditioned them through predation.
event_triggers: On entry: Your footsteps dislodge a stone that falls into the chasm. You count to seven before it hits something. Then it hits something else. The second impact sounds wet.

content_hints.description: The Flooded Gallery opens where two chasms intersect -- a cathedral-sized cavern where water pools knee-deep in the low season and neck-deep when the highstorms drain. The walls carry tide marks at five different heights, each stained a different mineral color. Bones float in the shallows -- human, Parshendi, and things with too many joints.
content_hints.read_aloud: The chasms converge in a cathedral of wet stone, its ceiling lost in darkness above. Water pools at your knees -- cold, mineral-thick, tasting of copper and time. Tide marks stripe the walls at five heights, each a different mineral stain: iron-red, copper-green, chalk-white, and two colors you have no name for. Bones float in the shallows. Some are human. Some are Parshendi. Some have too many joints and not enough symmetry to be either.

enemies.name: Whitespine (juvenile)
enemies.notes: Amphibious. Advantage on attacks while in water.

content_hints.investigation_success: The soldier's pack contains a sealed message tube. Inside: a report from a Sadeas scout describing 'structures of worked stone at the chasm's deepest point, predating known civilization.' The report was never delivered.
content_hints.perception_success: The water level is rising. Not slowly -- visibly, an inch every few minutes. A storm above is draining into the chasms. You have perhaps an hour before this gallery becomes fully submerged.
event_triggers: On entry: The water level is rising. Slowly, but visibly. A storm above is draining into the chasms. Bones shift in the current like pointing fingers.

content_hints.description: Chasmfiend bones arch overhead -- the ribcage of an adult specimen wedged between the chasm walls forty feet up, where it died trying to climb free. The skeleton has calcified into the stone itself, and cremlings have built entire colonies in its eye sockets. Parshendi ceremonial carvings cover the creature's carapace.
content_hints.read_aloud: A ribcage arches overhead like the vault of a bone cathedral -- the skeleton of an adult chasmfiend wedged between the chasm walls forty feet above, calcified into the stone where it died climbing. Cremling colonies glow in its eye sockets like lanterns. Parshendi carvings cover every inch of its carapace -- not vandalism, but veneration. This creature was worshipped. The hatchlings below its remains regard you with their parent's predator intelligence.

enemies.name: Chasmfiend Hatchling
enemies.notes: Pack tactics. Advantage when an ally is adjacent to the target.

content_hints.investigation_success: The Parshendi carvings on the chasmfiend carapace are not a death memorial. They are instructions -- a ceremony for binding a chasmfiend's gemheart after death. The Parshendi understood these creatures far better than the Alethi realize.
content_hints.perception_success: The hatchlings freeze when they sense you -- not in fear, but evaluation. Their compound eyes track your movements with coordinated precision. They are communicating. Silently.
event_triggers: On entry: The hatchlings freeze when they sense you -- not afraid, but evaluating. They have their parent's predator intelligence. One clicks its mandibles twice. The other responds with three.

content_hints.description: The Stormlight Crystal Cave. Natural gemstone formations thrust from every surface -- amethyst, heliodor, sapphire, ruby -- each one drinking light and exhaling it as Stormlight. The air is dense with investiture, and your skin prickles with static charge. A Radiant could feast here. The crystals hum at frequencies that make your vision blur.
content_hints.read_aloud: The cave erupts in color. Amethyst pillars thrust from the floor like frozen geysers. Sapphire clusters bloom from the walls. Ruby veins thread the ceiling in arterial patterns. Every crystal drinks your torchlight and exhales it as Stormlight -- a cold, electric luminescence that prickles your skin and stands your hair on end. The air is so dense with Investiture that breathing feels like swallowing lightning. Your dun spheres begin to glow.
content_hints.investigation_success: The crystal formations are not natural. They follow the same geometric patterns as the Oathgate glyphs -- this cave is a Stormlight conduit, channeling energy from the highstorms above into the structures below. The chasms are not wounds in the land. They are veins.
content_hints.perception_success: Deep in the crystal forest, a single formation glows with a light that is not Stormlight -- warmer, golden, and accompanied by a sound like distant singing. It feels like it is waiting for someone specific.
event_triggers: On entry: Stormlight floods your body unbidden -- cuts close, bruises fade, and for a moment you understand why the Radiants were willing to die for this. Then the feeling passes, and the loss of it is worse than any wound.

content_hints.description: An Ancient Oathgate Fragment -- a section of worked stone utterly unlike the natural chasm walls, carved with the ten glyph-patterns of the Radiant Orders. It juts from the rock as if the chasm itself broke through a structure that was here first. The stone is warm to the touch, and Stormlight seeps from its seams like blood from a wound.
content_hints.read_aloud: The chasm wall changes. Natural stone gives way to worked stone -- seamless, jointless, carved with ten glyph patterns you recognize from the Oathgate above. This structure was here first. The chasm broke through it, not the other way around. Stormlight seeps from the seams like luminous blood, and the stone is warm beneath your palm. A Midnight Essence peels itself from the wall's shadow, its form a hole in the light shaped like something that was once human.

enemies.name: Midnight Essence
enemies.notes: Shadow creature. Resistant to non-Stormlight weapons.
special: Shadow Meld: Can merge with shadows to become invisible for 1 round

content_hints.investigation_success: The Oathgate fragment contains a functional transit mechanism. It does not connect to another location on Roshar. It connects to the Cognitive Realm. The ancient Radiants had a backdoor.
content_hints.perception_success: The glyph patterns illuminate in sequence when the Midnight Essence is destroyed, spelling a word in a language predating human civilization on Roshar. Ellista's research from Urithiru would translate it as 'Remember.'
event_triggers: On entry: The Oathgate fragment responds to your presence -- glyphs illuminate in sequence, tracing a pattern that spells a word in a language predating human civilization on Roshar. The Midnight Essence stirs.

content_hints.description: The Chasmfiend Nest. The chasm widens into a bowl-shaped cavern strewn with shed carapace plates the size of wagon wheels. The walls are scarred with claw marks three inches deep. At the center, a fully grown chasmfiend curls around its chrysalis, compound eyes tracking your every movement with alien intelligence. It does not attack immediately. It waits.
content_hints.read_aloud: The chasm opens into a cathedral of stone and chitin. Carapace plates the size of wagon wheels litter the floor -- shed armor from a creature that fills this space the way a heart fills a chest. The adult chasmfiend curls around its chrysalis at the chamber's center, sixty feet of armored legs and mandibles and compound eyes that track your torchlight with alien patience. It does not charge. It does not roar. It waits. And in the waiting, you understand: this is its world. You are the intruder.

enemies.name: Adult Chasmfiend
enemies.notes: Legendary creature. Carapace grants damage resistance to non-magical weapons. Gemheart glow reveals weak points when below half HP.
special: Mandible Crush: 2d10+5 damage, target is grappled
Carapace Slam: All adjacent targets take 1d8+3 impact damage
Gemheart Pulse: When below half HP, heals 10 HP once per combat

content_hints.investigation_success: The dormant Shardblade was not dropped here by accident. It is embedded in the chrysalis -- the chasmfiend grew around it. The blade is being preserved, not abandoned. Something planted it here deliberately, four thousand years ago.
content_hints.perception_success: The adult chasmfiend is protecting its young, not guarding territory. If you approach the hatchlings, it attacks. If you back away from them, it holds position. There may be a path through this encounter that does not require killing a mother defending her children.
event_triggers: On entry: The adult chasmfiend rises to its full height -- sixty feet of armored legs and mandibles and eyes that have no right to hold the intelligence they do. The hatchlings scatter behind it. It is protecting them. The decision you make next will define you.
